name: Hopelink
description: Flexible financial assistance can help cover costs for prescriptions, medical bills, water and sewer bills, car repair bills, and other needs based on an unexpected financial shock. Funds from this program along with eviction prevention and first month’s rent are applied directly to the bill the client has been approved to receive financial assistance for.
access: To access, fill out the online form or call the service center
notes: Only serves those parts of eligible ZIPs within King County. Lottery based each month, and must reapply monthly to stay on the list

name: Hopelink
description: When a low-income family is establishing a new home – often for the first time – Hopelink can help to pay a portion of the high costs of settling into a new apartment. Move-in costs range from the first month’s rent to deposits. This kind of assistance can make a difference for a family at risk of losing their housing.
access: To access, fill out the online form or call the service center
notes: Only serves those parts of eligible ZIPs within King County. Lottery based each month, and must reapply monthly to stay on the list

name: Hopelink
description: When a family is in danger of losing its home due to receiving a 14- or 30-day pay or vacate notice, Hopelink can help to provide a portion of the funds needed to prevent eviction. Preventing homelessness costs at least five times less than helping a family regain stable housing after it’s lost. In addition to preventing eviction, Hopelink’s Financial Assistance program may be able to help with move-in costs.
access: To access, fill out the online form or call the service center
notes: Only serves those parts of eligible ZIPs within King County. Lottery based each month, and must reapply monthly to stay on the list

name: Hopelink
description: At no cost, clients can shop at the market as they would in a typical grocery store by choosing the items that best fit their household needs.
access: All shoppers must be enrolled in the Food Assistance Program. Options to enroll or renew an expired enrollment include an online application, scheduled phone appointment, or in-person appointment. Once enrolled, clients can schedule a time to shop either by calling their local center or in-person during a food shopping visit.
notes: Appointments are encouraged. Walk-ins are welcome during the last hour of market hour, which are available at the listed website

name: St. Vincent de Paul of Seattle/King County
description: St. Vincent de Paul Helpline assists people who need help with basic needs like rent, food, utilities and clothing.
access: Call the helpline M-F from 8-3, or apply online
notes: Only serves King County. May receive a call or visit from trained volunteers to help meet any other needs

name: United Way of King County
description: Rental assistance, but not move-in assistance. Must owe back-rent at current address.
access: Fill out the Client Intake Form to see if you’re eligible. Be sure to save your Record ID after submitting! After applying, watch your email closely. You may hear from United Way or a partner organization. Check your spam/junk folder and use the same email and phone number when following up.
notes: Only serves King County. Processes all applications, but does not guarantee assistance. May take quite a while.

name: United Way of King County
description: Help with filling out tax forms during tax season. Both virtual and in person.
access: Navigate to the appropriate portion of the website

name: Basic Food
description: Also caled EBT or SNAP
access: To access, fill out the online form or call

name: SCL Utility Discount Program
description: The City of Seattle can help people who are struggling to pay their electricity, water, and waste utility bills. Eligible households can enroll in the City of Seattle’s Utility Discount Program (UDP), which offers a 60% discount on Seattle City Light bills and a 50% discount on Seattle Public Utilities bills.
access: To access, complete the online enrollment form
notes: For households with utilities in their name in Seattle

name: PSE Utility Discount Program
description: Bill Discount Rate (BDR) program provides you with ongoing help on your monthly energy bill. Depending on your household income and household size, you can save 5% to 45% a month on your bill.
access: To access, complete the online enrollment form
notes: For households with utilities in their name with PSE
